MULTY MEDICAL SKILLED NURSING FACILITY

CCN: 405034 · RIO PIEDRAS, PR 00935 · San Juan County

OverviewRatingsInspectionsDeficienciesOwnership & ChainFacility FeaturesAll Data

Overview

Address
AMERICO MIRANDA AVE ENTRADA PRINCIPAL CENTRO, RIO PIEDRAS, PR 00935
Phone
7877540194
Certified beds

Provider type

—
Rating not available

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
New facility — too new to be rated.
— Not Available
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
New facility — too new to be rated.
— Not Available
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
New facility — too new to be rated.
— Not Available
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
New facility — too new to be rated.
— Not Available

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-09-25 — — — — — —

Deficiencies (69)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

18 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
51 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0553 Allow resident to participate in the development and implementation of his or her person-centered plan of care. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0559 Honor the resident's right to share a room with spouse or roommate of choice and receive written notice before a change is made. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0560 Protect a residents' right to refuse some types of non-requested transfers within the nursing home. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0561 Honor the resident's right to and the facility must promote and facilitate resident self-determination through support of resident choice. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0562 Provide immediate access to any resident. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0563 Honor the resident's right to receive visitors of his or her choosing, at the time of his or her choosing. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0564 Inform each resident of his or her visitation rights and ensure that all visitors enjoy equal visitation privileges. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0566 1) Protect residents from being forced to work at the nursing home, or 2) let residents work if they want to. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0567 Honor the resident's right to manage his or her financial affairs. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0572 Give residents a notice of rights, rules, services and charges. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0573 Let each resident or the resident's legal representative access or purchase copies of all the resident's records. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0574 The resident has the right to receive notices in a format and a language he or she understands. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0575 Post a list of names, addresses, and telephone numbers of all pertinent State agencies and advocacy groups and a statement that the resident may file a complaint with the State Survey Agency. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0577 Allow residents to easily view the nursing home's survey results and communicate with advocate agencies. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2024-09-25 2024-10-03
0579 Provide information about how to apply for and use Medicare and Medicaid benefits. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0586 Not prohibit or in any way discourage a resident from communicating with federal, state, or local officials. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0602 Protect each resident from the wrongful use of the resident's belongings or money. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0603 Protect each resident from separation (from other residents, his/her room, or confinement to his/her room). F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0604 Ensure that each resident is free from the use of physical restraints, unless needed for medical treatment. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0605 Prevent the use of unnecessary psychotropic medications or use medications that may restrain a resident's ability to function. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. C 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0620 Not require residents to give up Medicare or Medicaid benefits, or pay privately as a condition of admission; and must tell residents what care they do not provide. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0622 Not transfer or discharge a resident without an adequate reason; and must provide documentation and convey specific information when a resident is transferred or discharged. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0624 Prepare residents for a safe transfer or discharge from the nursing home. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0625 Notify the resident or the resident’s representative in writing how long the nursing home will hold the resident’s bed in cases of transfer to a hospital or therapeutic leave. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0626 Permit a resident to return to the nursing home after hospitalization or therapeutic leave that exceeds bed-hold policy. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2024-09-25 2024-09-25
0685 Assist a resident in gaining access to vision and hearing services. C 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0687 Provide appropriate foot care. C 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0691 Provide appropriate colostomy, urostomy, or ileostomy care/services for a resident who requires such services. C 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. C 2024-09-25 2024-11-14
0696 Provide appropriate care/assistance for a resident with a prosthesis. C 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. C 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0699 Provide care or services that was trauma informed and/or culturally competent. C 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0700 Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. C 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. F 2024-09-25 2024-11-20
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. C 2024-09-25 2024-09-30
0740 Ensure each resident must receive and the facility must provide necessary behavioral health care and services. F 2024-09-25 2024-09-30

Showing 50 most recent of 69. See the All Data CSV for the full list.

Ownership & Corporate Structure

Owner / manager individuals (4)

Name Role Association
CONDE STERLING, TANIA 5% OR GREATER DIRECT OWNERSHIP INTEREST since 09/01/2016
CONDE STERLING, TANIA OPERATIONAL/MANAGERIAL CONTROL since 09/01/2016
SEPULVEDA-IRIZARRY, FERNANDO CORPORATE DIRECTOR since 10/28/2018
CONDE STERLING, TANIA CORPORATE OFFICER since 09/01/2016

Source: CMS Nursing Home Ownership. Percent ownership is rarely disclosed — CMS only requires it for specific roles.

Methodology & sources

Deficiencies 0699 — description Provide care or services that was trauma informed and/or culturally competent. 0699.description
Deficiencies 0699 — scope_severity C 0699.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0699 — survey_date 2024-09-25 0699.survey_date
Deficiencies 0699 — tag 0699 0699.tag
Deficiencies 0700 — correction_date 2024-09-30 0700.correction_date
Deficiencies 0700 — description Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. 0700.description
Deficiencies 0700 — scope_severity C 0700.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0700 — survey_date 2024-09-25 0700.survey_date
Deficiencies 0700 — tag 0700 0700.tag
Deficiencies 0727 — correction_date 2024-11-20 0727.correction_date
Deficiencies 0727 — description Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. 0727.description
Deficiencies 0727 — scope_severity F 0727.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0727 — survey_date 2024-09-25 0727.survey_date
Deficiencies 0727 — tag 0727 0727.tag
Deficiencies 0732 — correction_date 2024-09-30 0732.correction_date
Deficiencies 0732 — description Post nurse staffing information every day. 0732.description
Deficiencies 0732 — scope_severity C 0732.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0732 — survey_date 2024-09-25 0732.survey_date
Deficiencies 0732 — tag 0732 0732.tag
Deficiencies 0740 — correction_date 2024-09-30 0740.correction_date
Deficiencies 0740 — description Ensure each resident must receive and the facility must provide necessary behavioral health care and services. 0740.description
Deficiencies 0740 — scope_severity F 0740.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0740 — survey_date 2024-09-25 0740.survey_date
Deficiencies 0740 — tag 0740 0740.tag
Deficiencies 0741 — correction_date 2024-09-30 0741.correction_date
Deficiencies 0741 — description Ensure that the facility has sufficient staff members who possess the competencies and skills to meet the behavioral health needs of residents. 0741.description
Deficiencies 0741 — scope_severity F 0741.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0741 — survey_date 2024-09-25 0741.survey_date
Deficiencies 0741 — tag 0741 0741.tag
Deficiencies 0756 — correction_date 2024-10-29 0756.correction_date
Deficiencies 0756 — description Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. 0756.description
Deficiencies 0756 — scope_severity F 0756.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0756 — survey_date 2024-09-25 0756.survey_date
Deficiencies 0756 — tag 0756 0756.tag
Deficiencies 0757 — correction_date 2024-10-29 0757.correction_date
Deficiencies 0757 — description Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. 0757.description
Deficiencies 0757 — scope_severity C 0757.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0757 — survey_date 2024-09-25 0757.survey_date
Deficiencies 0757 — tag 0757 0757.tag

Deficiencies 0790 — description Provide routine and 24-hour emergency dental care for each resident. 0790.description

Deficiencies 0790 — tag 0790 0790.tag
Deficiencies 0800 — correction_date 2024-11-04 0800.correction_date
Deficiencies 0800 — description Provide each resident with a nourishing, palatable, well-balanced diet that meets his or her daily nutritional and special dietary needs. 0800.description
Deficiencies 0800 — scope_severity F 0800.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0800 — survey_date 2024-09-25 0800.survey_date

Deficiencies 0804 — description Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. 0804.description

Deficiencies 0812 — description Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. 0812.description
